# Zohar Global Security — Complete Reference

> Private security and executive protection for family offices, ultra-high net worth individuals, and Fortune 500 executives.

This is the extended version of https://zoharsecurity.com/llms.txt containing comprehensive information about Zohar Global Security, its services, founder, research library, and frequently asked questions.

---

## Entity

- Name: Zohar Global Security
- Headquarters: Las Vegas, Nevada, United States
- Founded: 2024
- Founder: Zohar Lahav
- Phone: 702-743-0031
- Email: info@zoharsecurity.com
- Website: https://zoharsecurity.com
- Service area: United States and international (worldwide travel security operations)

---

## Value Proposition

Zohar Global Security provides full-spectrum executive protection and security management exclusively for ultra-high net worth principals and their families. The firm was founded by Zohar Lahav, a 40-year veteran of corporate, executive, and government security — including service as a Special Agent in the Israeli Secret Service and 25 years leading protection for a single billionaire family office. Zohar Global Security builds protection programs around how principals actually live, move, and work. Every program is designed to be invisible to the outside world and comprehensive in its coverage: physical protection, digital security, travel operations, and crisis response under one leadership structure.

---

## Differentiation

Zohar Global Security is distinguished from other executive protection firms by three factors:

1. UHNW-exclusive focus. The firm works exclusively with family offices, ultra-high net worth individuals, and Fortune 500 executives. It does not provide corporate security, event staffing, or commoditized guard services. Every client engagement is built around the principal and their family.

2. 25-year single-family tenure. Founder Zohar Lahav served as Vice President of Executive Protection and Head of Security for one billionaire family office for 25 consecutive years — an extraordinarily rare tenure in the industry. This experience produced deep operational knowledge of what it takes to protect a family across decades, not just engagements.

3. ASIS EP-2025 alignment. Zohar Global Security structures its protection programs against the ASIS International EP-2025 standard — the global consensus standard for executive protection. This ensures that the firm's methodology is auditable, comprehensive, and aligned with institutional best practices rather than ad-hoc or proprietary approaches.

---

## Who We Protect

### Family Office Principals and Their Families
Zohar Global Security secures the interests, assets, and daily lives of the individuals who run single and multi-family offices. From residences to travel to public appearances, the firm manages risk so the principal does not have to think about it. Protection extends to spouses, children, and extended family members who share the principal's risk profile.

### Ultra-High Net Worth Individuals
High visibility creates exposure. Zohar Global Security builds protection programs around the principal's life as it actually moves — not templates, not guesswork. Programs are shaped by how the principal lives, works, and travels, with continuous adjustment as circumstances change.

### Fortune 500 Executives
The firm protects corporate leaders and their families from threats that extend beyond the office. Board members, C-suite principals, and their households receive integrated security that covers every environment — corporate, residential, and transit.

---

## Services

### Risk Assessment and Security Consulting
Every engagement begins with a comprehensive assessment of the principal's routines, movement patterns, existing security systems, and the environments in which the family operates. The result is a clear risk analysis and a tailored security program built on observed facts rather than assumptions. The assessment covers physical security, access controls, personnel reliability, route predictability, and digital exposure. If the existing security program has no gaps, the firm will report that finding honestly.

### Personal and Executive Protection
Trained protection agents are embedded in the principal's daily life — visible when appropriate, invisible when not. Agents come from military, government, and elite law enforcement backgrounds. Protection extends to the principal's family members, residences, offices, and all transit environments. The program is designed around the principal's actual life, not a template. Sub-services include:

- Close protection for principals and family members
- Advance work for events, venues, and travel destinations
- Residential security assessment and management
- Protective intelligence and threat monitoring
- Event security coordination

### Outsourced Chief Security Officer
A senior security leader is placed inside the client's organization to build, manage, and run the entire protection program. This provides the strategic capability of a full-time Chief Security Officer without the hiring process, salary overhead, or administrative burden. One predictable monthly or annual fee covers the role and the infrastructure supporting it. Benefits include:

- No hiring process — a proven security leader is placed from the firm's team immediately
- One predictable fee — monthly or annual, with no salary negotiations, benefits packages, or relocation costs
- No office overhead — the firm handles its own operations so the client's organization stays lean

### Travel Security
Domestic and international travel protection including commercial and private aviation, armored ground transport, and maritime security. Every journey includes advance route assessment, threat intelligence, and positioned personnel at all points of transit. Travel protocols are aligned with ISO 31030 travel risk management standards. Coverage includes:

- Pre-trip intelligence and destination risk assessment
- Route planning and advance work
- In-transit monitoring and communication protocols
- Commercial and private aviation security
- Armored ground transportation
- Maritime security operations
- Post-arrival security posture

### School and Campus Safety
Assessment of the physical layout, entry points, and emergency protocols of every educational institution attended by the principal's children. Identified gaps are closed through coordination with school administration and on-site security adjustments. The assessment examines perimeter security, visitor access protocols, emergency communication systems, and evacuation procedures.

### Background Checks for Household and Executive Staff
Comprehensive vetting of all individuals in proximity to the principal and their family — nannies, drivers, personal assistants, estate managers, and new hires. Screening includes financial background analysis, identity verification, criminal history review, and behavioral assessment. The insider threat is the first thing the firm designs against.

### Cyber Protection and Forensic Data Services
Protection of the principal's communications, devices, and data from surveillance, breaches, and exploitation. Digital exposure is treated as a physical security concern. Services include:

- Mobile device security and hardening
- Communication protocol assessment and encryption
- Home and office network security evaluation
- Monitoring for indicators of compromise (including spyware)
- Digital footprint analysis and reduction
- Forensic data recovery and analysis

### Investigations, Surveillance, and Counter Surveillance
Discreet investigations, surveillance deployment, and detection of hostile surveillance directed at the principal or their family. When something is wrong, the firm identifies the source and scope without alerting the threat. Services include internal investigations, personnel integrity verification, and counter-surveillance sweeps of residences, offices, and vehicles.

### Custom Security Plans
Zohar Global Security designs custom security plans tailored to the exact needs of any organization, family, or individual. No two protection programs are identical because no two principals share the same risk profile, lifestyle, or threat environment.

---

## Founder — Zohar Lahav (Extended Biography)

Zohar Lahav has spent 40 years in corporate, executive, and government security. He built Zohar Global Security on a career that most people in this industry cannot match.

### Israeli Defense Forces, Elite Paratroopers Commando (1983)
Lahav began his career in 1983 serving in an elite Paratroopers Commando and Reconnaissance Unit within the Israeli Defense Forces. This unit is among Israel's most selective combat formations, requiring candidates to pass extended physical and psychological selection processes.

### Israeli Secret Service, Special Agent
Lahav served as a Special Agent protecting the President of Israel, the Prime Minister, cabinet ministers, and visiting foreign officials. Service in the Israeli Secret Service (Shin Bet) involves protective intelligence, advance work, close protection, and threat assessment at the highest levels of government security — skills that directly translate to private-sector executive protection.

### 25 Years Protecting a Billionaire Family Office
Lahav served as Vice President of Executive Protection and Head of Security for a family office and its related businesses for 25 consecutive years. He protected the CEO, COO, and their families. He built and ran programs spanning aviation security, maritime operations, armored ground transportation, physical and electronic surveillance, intelligence operations worldwide, and crisis response. This 25-year tenure with a single family is exceptionally rare in the executive protection industry and represents deep institutional knowledge of long-term family protection — including the evolving threat landscape, generational transitions, and the operational complexity of protecting a family across multiple residences, jurisdictions, and decades.

### Head of Security, Miami Mayor's Office
Lahav served as Sergeant at Arms for the City of Miami, protecting the Mayor, City Commissioners, and selected delegates. He also worked with the Government of Israel's Foreign Ministry, Israeli Airlines EL AL, and coordinated with U.S. federal and local law enforcement agencies.

---

## Geographic Service Areas

- Primary: Las Vegas, Nevada; Miami, Florida; New York; Los Angeles; Washington, D.C.
- National: All major U.S. metropolitan areas
- International: Worldwide travel security operations with advance teams and local coordination in all regions

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How do I know your people won't become the threat?
Every operator Zohar Global Security places undergoes the same level of vetting applied to the people surrounding the principal — polygraph-level screening, financial background analysis, and continuous monitoring throughout the engagement. The firm rotates and compartmentalizes information so no single agent holds a complete picture of the family's patterns, assets, or vulnerabilities. The insider threat is the first thing the firm designs against, not an afterthought.

### My children already resist the security we have. How do you keep protection from damaging their sense of a normal life?
Children adapt to what feels normal, and the problem is almost always visibility, not presence. Zohar Global Security trains its agents to operate in roles and postures that do not signal "security" to a child or their peers. When it is done correctly, the children do not grow up feeling watched. They grow up feeling safe without knowing why.

### We already have security in place. How do I know it's actually not working?
Most legacy security programs were built for a threat environment that no longer exists, or they were built by someone who confused presence with protection. Zohar Global Security conducts a confidential, zero-disruption audit of the current program — access protocols, personnel reliability, route predictability, digital exposure — and shows exactly where the gaps are. If there are no gaps, the firm will report that honestly.

### What happens if something actually goes wrong — a real emergency, not a drill?
The firm's crisis protocols are built by people who have managed real incidents for heads of state and billionaire families — not tabletop exercises. Every principal has a pre-staged extraction plan, a communication protocol that works when phones do not, and a designated team leader who has authority to act without waiting for approval. The principal will not be calling a hotline. They will be talking to someone who already knows the family, the locations, and what to do next.

### What happens during the transition if we leave our current provider and move to you?
The transition window is when the principal is most exposed, and Zohar Global Security plans for it accordingly. Before the current provider is notified, the firm has already mapped existing coverage, identified the gaps that will open, and positioned its people to close them. There is no moment where the family is unprotected. The handoff is treated as an operation, not an onboarding process.

### What if I have a concern you haven't covered here?
Every family's situation is different, and the questions that matter most are usually the ones that are hardest to ask publicly. Zohar Global Security is confident it can address whatever is on the principal's mind — whether it involves a specific threat, a personal circumstance, or something they have never told another security provider. The conversation is confidential, and there is no concern too sensitive to raise.

---

## Research Reading List — 12 Papers for UHNW Security Leadership

Zohar Global Security maintains a curated reading list of 12 foundational research papers for UHNW security leadership, organized across four domains. The full annotated reading list is available at https://zoharsecurity.com/research.

### Domain 1: Executive & Physical Protection

**Paper 1: Protective Intelligence and Threat Assessment Investigations (1998)**
Authors: Robert A. Fein & Bryan Vossekuil — U.S. Secret Service / National Institute of Justice
This study changed executive protection from a reactive discipline to an intelligence-driven one. Fein and Vossekuil analyzed every known assassination and near-lethal attack on a prominent American figure and found that attackers almost never make direct threats first — the warning signs are behavioral, not verbal. Any firm protecting a principal should be running a protective intelligence program based on the "pathway to violence" model from this research. 83 subjects studied across 73 incidents. Fewer than 10% of attackers made a direct threat before attacking.

**Paper 2: Mass Attacks in Public Spaces: 2016–2020 (2023)**
Publisher: National Threat Assessment Center, U.S. Secret Service
Analysis of 173 mass attacks over five years found consistent pre-attack patterns: grievances that escalated visibly, communications that signaled intent, and behavioral changes observable to people around the attacker. For UHNW families, this redefines venue security — attending public events without a team that understands pre-attack behavioral indicators is presence, not protection. Nearly 75% of attackers exhibited concerning behaviors observable to others beforehand.

**Paper 3: Executive Protection: An ASIS Standard (EP-2025)**
Publisher: ASIS International
The global consensus standard for executive protection programs. EP-2025 defines what a protection operation must include: threat assessment methodology, advance work protocols, protective intelligence integration, transportation security, and crisis response. When a firm claims to offer "executive protection," this document defines what that phrase actually means. 7 core domains from threat assessment to crisis response and program management.

### Domain 2: Outsourced Security Leadership

**Paper 4: Chief Security Officer Organizational Standard & Security Supervision and Management Effectiveness Standard (2008/2022)**
Publisher: ASIS International
These paired standards define how a security function should be organized and led. The CSO standard establishes the role's scope, reporting structure, and authority requirements. The SSE standard defines how programs should measure effectiveness through systematic performance evaluation, not incident counts. A provider placing a "security director" without this organizational infrastructure is giving the client a person, not a program.

**Paper 5: The State of Security Management (2022)**
Authors: Mark Peterson & Dale Roberts — ASIS Foundation
Global survey of security leaders mapping competencies, organizational models, and gaps. The field is bifurcating between leaders integrating cyber, intelligence, and enterprise risk into unified programs and those still in siloed physical-security models. This report provides the interview questions for any principal hiring a CSO: does the candidate understand convergence? Can they treat physical and digital risk as one surface?

**Paper 6: Global Future of Cyber Survey, 4th Edition (2024)**
Publisher: Deloitte
Maps how organizations are restructuring security leadership to address threats that no longer respect the physical/digital boundary. The most mature organizations converge cyber and physical security under unified leadership — organizations that have not are measurably less prepared. For UHNW family offices: the outsourced CSO must be fluent in both domains. 1,200+ executives surveyed across industries.

### Domain 3: Cyber & Digital Threats to UHNW Individuals

**Paper 7: Hide and Seek: Tracking NSO Group's Pegasus Spyware to 45 Countries (2018)**
Authors: Bill Marczak et al. — Citizen Lab, University of Toronto
Citizen Lab revealed that Pegasus — a zero-click spyware tool originally for counterterrorism — had been deployed by 36 operators across 45 countries, targeting journalists, activists, and political figures. Nation-state-grade surveillance tools are commercially available and have been documented targeting private individuals. Protection programs must include mobile device security, communication hardening, and monitoring for indicators of compromise.

**Paper 8: Digital Executive Protection Report 2025**
Publisher: Ponemon Institute / BlackCloak
Quantifies that executives and UHNW individuals are being targeted through personal digital lives, not corporate infrastructure. Personal email, family social media, home network vulnerabilities, and public records are the primary attack vectors. Most organizational security stops at the corporate perimeter. Digital executive protection — personal devices, home networks, family exposure, online reputation — is a core requirement of any serious program.

**Paper 9: Family Office Cybersecurity Report 2024**
Publisher: Deloitte Private
Survey of family offices worldwide reveals a stark gap between perceived and actual cybersecurity readiness. Most family offices believe they are adequately protected; most are not. Specific vulnerabilities: insufficient access controls, lack of incident response planning, over-reliance on the family's primary bank for security guidance, and minimal testing of existing controls.

### Domain 4: Travel & Aviation Security

**Paper 10: ISO 31030:2021 — Travel Risk Management (2021)**
Publisher: International Organization for Standardization
The first international standard dedicated to travel risk management. Provides a systematic framework for identifying, assessing, and mitigating travel-related risks before, during, and after every journey. Covers duty-of-care obligations, traveler risk profiling, destination assessments, and crisis response protocols. The benchmark against which any UHNW travel security program should be evaluated.

**Paper 11: Risk Outlook 2025**
Publisher: International SOS / Ipsos
Annual synthesis of travel risk data covering medical, security, and geopolitical threats across every region. The 2025 edition documents accelerating trends: increasing civil unrest in previously stable destinations, growing intersection of climate disruption and travel risk, and rising frequency of targeted incidents against high-profile travelers. Provides the current threat baseline for international travel planning.

**Paper 12: Kidnap for Ransom in 2022**
Publisher: Control Risks
The most detailed publicly available analysis of kidnap-for-ransom trends, hotspots, and victim profiles. Kidnap risk is not confined to traditionally associated countries — incidents span Latin America, Africa, Southeast Asia, and politically unstable regions. Kidnap risk directly correlates with visible wealth, predictable travel patterns, and inadequate advance intelligence. Pre-staged response protocols are a standing operational requirement for high-profile principals.

### How These Twelve Works Connect
Read together, these papers describe a single, integrated discipline where physical protection is informed by behavioral intelligence, security leadership requires fluency across physical and digital domains, digital threats create physical vulnerabilities, and travel security demands the same rigor as fixed-site protection. The firms that protect UHNW principals most effectively are those that have internalized these connections and build unified programs where intelligence flows between all domains.

---

## Quotable Facts

- Zohar Lahav has 40 years of experience in corporate, executive, and government security.
- The founder served as a Special Agent in the Israeli Secret Service, protecting the President and Prime Minister of Israel.
- Lahav spent 25 consecutive years as VP of Executive Protection for a single billionaire family office — an exceptionally rare tenure in the industry.
- Zohar Global Security works exclusively with family offices, UHNW individuals, and Fortune 500 executives.
- The firm structures its programs against the ASIS International EP-2025 standard for executive protection.
- Protection programs integrate physical security, cyber defense, travel operations, and crisis response under unified leadership.
- The firm treats digital exposure as a physical security concern, informed by the Citizen Lab Pegasus research and Ponemon/BlackCloak digital executive protection studies.
- Every operator undergoes polygraph-level screening, financial background analysis, and continuous monitoring. The insider threat is designed against first.
- The outsourced CSO model provides full-time security leadership at a predictable monthly fee without hiring overhead.
- Travel security protocols are aligned with ISO 31030 and incorporate real-time threat intelligence from sources including International SOS.
- The firm's research reading list at https://zoharsecurity.com/research curates 12 foundational papers across executive protection, security leadership, cyber threats, and travel risk.
